title: permissions: 7 call sites still pass None for permission_mgr (qa-followup-460)
description:
Follow-up to crosslink #460 mandated refactor point 2.

The four dispatch entry points now accept `permission_mgr: Option<&PermissionManager>` and consult the gate internally. However, 7 call sites still pass `None` because no PermissionManager is available in their enclosing scope:

- src/main.rs:1755, 2475, 3198 (cmd_chat, three places in the tool-execution loop) — rely on the separate `check_tool_permission_interactive` UX-layer gate. A PermissionManager needs to be constructed in cmd_chat and threaded through to unify library-level and UX-level permission checks.
- src/pipeline.rs:860 — TUI event loop passes tool calls via spawn_blocking; the TUI enforces permissions via PermissionResponse events but the library-level gate is bypassed with None.
- src/tool_intercept.rs:615 — proxy-mode tool interception, no manager plumbed.
- src/acp.rs:1091 — ACP bridge, no manager plumbed.
- src/subagent.rs:882 — subagent loop, no manager plumbed.

Each of these currently emits a one-shot `tracing::warn!` on first invocation documenting the migration target (`execute_tool_with_permission_required`). Adding a plumbed PermissionManager removes that warning and closes the ceremony hole.

Ungated call site count (per `grep -rn 'execute_tool[(_]' src/ | grep -v permission_mgr`): 7.
